degraded.reason: Pre-existing sweep-outbox warning from setup_iterate_worktree (trg-671bb0a2: a status append dropped by a prior merge) — unrelated to this change; non-fatal, the worktree was still created.

surface_verification.justification: Pure internal durability hardening: new shared durable_atomic_write primitive + ~20 delegating helpers. No UI/API/CLI surface is introduced or changed; written files are byte-identical to before (fsync is the only added behavior), so there is no user-observable or frontend contract change. Verified empirically by 13 primitive unit tests + a 5-writer cross-subsystem composition test + full shared/plugin/integration suites green.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 str/bytes/unicode/embedded-newline written verbatim and round-trip byte-identical
AC2 os.fsync of the temp file precedes os.replace (durability ordering)
AC3 parent-dir fsync is best-effort (OSError swallowed; Windows skips os.open)
AC4 os.replace failure unlinks tmp and re-raises; target never created
AC5 parent dirs created; wholesale overwrite; no .tmp leftover on success
AC6 every migrated helper keeps byte-identical output and routes through the primitive
behaviors.evidence: full shared suite 3414 passed + 4 plugin suites (run 162/project 59/build 84/security 417) + run_config_store round-trip tests unchanged

behaviors.behavior: AC7 full test suite green (no regression from the migration)
behaviors.evidence: shared 3414 + integration 147 + 722 plugin tests all passed; full-repo ruff clean

behaviors.behavior: INTEGRATION: writers across shared/lib, phase_quality, and shared/tools all compose on one durable primitive (fsync-before-replace + byte round-trip)
